tiles.name: Mediterranean Ave
tiles.funFact: The most affordable property in Atlantic City, perfect for first-time property owners!

tiles.name: Baltic Ave
tiles.funFact: Named after the Baltic Sea region, this street was home to many Eastern European immigrants.

tiles.name: Reading Railroad
tiles.funFact: Founded in 1833, the Reading Railroad was one of the first railroads in the United States!

tiles.name: Oriental Ave
tiles.funFact: This street celebrates Atlantic City's historic connection to Asian culture and trade.

tiles.name: Vermont Ave
tiles.funFact: Vermont, the Green Mountain State, is famous for its maple syrup and beautiful fall foliage.

tiles.name: Connecticut Ave
tiles.funFact: Connecticut is known as the Constitution State and is home to Yale University!

tiles.name: St. Charles Place
tiles.funFact: Named after a historic hotel, St. Charles Place was a popular destination in early Atlantic City.

tiles.name: Electric Company
tiles.funFact: Thomas Edison's invention of the light bulb in 1879 revolutionized the electric industry!

tiles.name: States Ave
tiles.funFact: This street represents the unity of all 50 states in America!

tiles.name: Virginia Ave
tiles.funFact: Virginia was home to 8 U.S. Presidents, more than any other state!

tiles.name: Pennsylvania Railroad
tiles.funFact: The Pennsylvania Railroad was once the largest railroad in the world by traffic and revenue!

tiles.name: St. James Place
tiles.funFact: St. James is the patron saint of pilgrims and travelers, perfect for a resort city!

tiles.name: Tennessee Ave
tiles.funFact: Tennessee is the birthplace of country music and home to the Grand Ole Opry!

tiles.name: New York Ave
tiles.funFact: New York City is home to the Statue of Liberty, a symbol of freedom worldwide!

tiles.name: Kentucky Ave
tiles.funFact: Kentucky is famous for the Kentucky Derby, the most exciting two minutes in sports!

tiles.name: Indiana Ave
tiles.funFact: Indiana is home to the Indianapolis 500, the world's largest single-day sporting event!

tiles.name: Illinois Ave
tiles.funFact: Chicago, Illinois is home to the world's first skyscraper, built in 1885!

tiles.name: B&O Railroad
tiles.funFact: The Baltimore & Ohio Railroad was the first common carrier railroad in America, founded in 1827!

tiles.name: Atlantic Ave
tiles.funFact: Atlantic Avenue runs parallel to the famous Atlantic City Boardwalk!

tiles.name: Ventnor Ave
tiles.funFact: Ventnor City neighbors Atlantic City and offers beautiful beaches and quieter shores.

tiles.name: Water Works
tiles.funFact: Atlantic City's water comes from the Kirkwood-Cohansey aquifer, one of the purest water sources!

tiles.name: Marvin Gardens
tiles.funFact: Actually spelled "Marven Gardens" in real life, located in nearby Margate City!

tiles.name: Pacific Ave
tiles.funFact: Pacific Avenue is one of Atlantic City's main thoroughfares, running through the heart of the city.

tiles.name: North Carolina Ave
tiles.funFact: North Carolina was the first state to declare independence from British rule!

tiles.name: Pennsylvania Ave
tiles.funFact: Pennsylvania Avenue in Washington D.C. connects the White House to the Capitol Building!

tiles.name: Short Line
tiles.funFact: The Shore Fast Line provided rapid transit service along the Jersey Shore in the early 1900s!

tiles.name: Park Place
tiles.funFact: One of the most prestigious addresses in Atlantic City, second only to Boardwalk!

tiles.name: Boardwalk
tiles.funFact: Atlantic City Boardwalk, built in 1870, was the first boardwalk in the United States!
